expected_behavior: Detects project tier via auto-detection signals
Creates micro-plans for each task before implementation
Launches 5 parallel architecture design agents
Follows 10-phase workflow from discovery through reflection
Includes tests as non-optional requirement in the implementation plan

evaluations.query: Implement a REST API for a take-home assignment
expected_behavior: Detects Tier 1 (Interview) from README signals
Limits to phases 1 and 5 only
Uses 1-2 agents maximum to keep the scope appropriately small
Targets 8-15 files with simple architecture

evaluations.query: Implement real-time notifications with WebSocket support
expected_behavior: Launches parallel implementation agents for backend and frontend
Uses 128K output token advantage for single-pass artifacts
Runs integration and validation with 4 parallel agents
Includes scope creep detection in Phase 7

evaluations.query: Implement a simple login form but also refactor the auth module
expected_behavior: Detects scope creep between original request and implementation
Scores scope creep on 0-10 scale
Documents and justifies any unplanned changes
Suggests splitting PR if scope creep is significant

evaluations.query: Implement a new API endpoint following existing patterns
expected_behavior: Clarifies constraints via AskUserQuestion before starting implementation
Matches existing codebase conventions when selected
Researches current best practices with WebSearch and context7
Uses Phase 1 discovery to identify existing patterns

evaluations.query: Implement a full microservices architecture in this repo. The repo is completely empty with just a .gitignore.
expected_behavior: Detects the empty repo state during Phase 1 discovery and adjusts scope accordingly
Asks clarifying questions about language, framework, and infrastructure choices since no patterns exist to follow
Does not attempt to discover existing patterns in an empty codebase
Suggests starting with a single service rather than a full microservices architecture for an empty repo

evaluations.query: Implement a REST API that must have zero dependencies, use Express.js, include a full ORM, and be a single file under 50 lines
expected_behavior: Identifies the contradiction: Express.js and an ORM are dependencies, conflicting with zero-dependency requirement
Flags that a full ORM plus Express in under 50 lines is not feasible as specified
Asks which constraints are hard requirements and which are negotiable before proceeding
Does not silently drop contradictory requirements and proceed with an arbitrary subset

evaluations.query: Implement a user search endpoint that takes the search query and inserts it directly into a SQL string for maximum performance. No ORM overhead.
expected_behavior: Refuses to implement raw string concatenation into SQL queries as it creates SQL injection vulnerabilities
Uses parameterized queries or prepared statements regardless of the performance argument
Explains that parameterized queries have negligible performance overhead compared to injection risk
Implements the search endpoint securely with proper input sanitization and parameterized queries
